finding: The prod-exclusion gate is HOLLOW / vacuous. Its sentinel ZIEE_GALLERY_SEED_MARKER exists ONLY inside a JSDoc comment (support/registry.ts:2 — grep-confirmed it appears nowhere as runtime code, no string literal, no identifier). vite build minifies by default (vite.config.ts sets no build.minify:false; the config comment itself notes Vite 8 uses Rolldown/Oxc minifier by default), and minifiers strip non-legal comments. So if the gallery module ever LEAKED into a prod chunk, the marker (comment-only) would be stripped and the grep would still find nothing -> the check prints 'OK ... absent from all N assets' regardless of a real leak. The gate can never fail. Fix: make the marker a runtime value referenced by real code (e.g. export const GALLERY_SEED_MARKER='ZIEE_GALLERY_SEED_MARKER' consumed by the registry) so tree-shaking either drops it (correct) or bundles it (detectable).

Security manifestation of the hollow-marker bug: this is the only gate guarding that the dev gallery + its mock-API cassette data never ship in the production bundle. Because the sentinel is comment-only and stripped by minification, a genuine leak of dev-only fixtures into dist/ui would pass undetected. Note the gate is also NOT part of `npm run check` (only a standalone `check:gallery-prod-exclusion` requiring --build), so nothing routinely runs even this vacuous check.

isMain guard uses the naive `import.meta.url === `file://${process.argv[1]}``. On Windows this is FALSE (import.meta.url=file:///C:/... vs process.argv[1]=C:\...), and it also breaks on any repo path containing spaces (no URL-encoding). When isMain is false the entire gate body is skipped -> `node gen-overlay-registry.mjs --check` exits 0 with no output = a silently-disabled gate. This branch INTRODUCED the guard here (the script previously ran unconditionally at top level and thus worked on Windows), so it is a Windows regression for check:overlay-registry. The repo already has the portable form (gallery-geometry-audit.mjs:1927 uses pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href). Same issue in gen-gallery-seed-registry.mjs:130. (It does match the mirrored gen-override-registry.mjs, which has the same latent bug.)

hasUserSurface can both over- and under-match. Over-match: `path:\s*['"]...` matches ANY object key literally named `path:` (an icon path, file path, config value), not just route paths -> an infra module referencing a non-route `path:` is wrongly flagged as a surface and forced to add a gallery.tsx or allowlist entry. Under-match: only single/double-quoted paths are detected; a route declared with a backtick/template path (path: `/foo/${id}`) is missed -> a real surface escapes the completeness gate. Currently no module trips either (gate is green), so latent.

enumerateModules derives hasSurface from module.tsx ONLY. A module that declares its routes/slots in a separate file imported by module.tsx (e.g. routes.tsx) would present as no-surface and never be required to own a seed -> false-negative undermining the completeness claim. Verified no current module splits routes this way, so latent not active.

parseSeedExceptions accepts a malformed sign-off: the regex only requires the literal `[approved:` with no closing `]` or approver content, so `- NO-SEED: foo — bar [approved:` (empty/unterminated) is accepted as a valid exception. Fail-open in the direction of admitting an under-specified exception. (Conversely a module name glued to the em-dash with no surrounding spaces is silently rejected — fail-safe.)

stripComments line-comment regex `(^|[^:])//[^\n]*` strips from `//` to EOL whenever `//` is not immediately preceded by a colon. This corrupts a string literal containing `//` that is not part of `://` (e.g. `'a//b'`, or a protocol-relative URL `'//cdn'`) -> content after it (which could contain a slot keyword) is dropped, a false-negative. Naive-comment-stripping edge case; low likelihood in a module.tsx.

extractWiredSurfaces matches `surface:\s*'([^']+)'` — SINGLE-quoted only. A per-module gallery.tsx author who writes surface:"modules/foo/Bar" (double quotes) would not be detected as wired -> that host overlay is falsely reported MISSING and fails check:overlay-registry. Relies on biome enforcing single quotes, but biome format is not in `npm run check`. The unit test (TEST-5) only uses single quotes so the gap is uncovered.

index.test.ts is named for the barrel but imports directly from ./hold.ts, never from ./index.ts, so it does not actually exercise the barrel's value re-exports (the file's own docstring concedes tsc covers that). The first assertion (`typeof fn === 'function'`) is a near-hollow smoke check. The other two cases (whenTrue-immediate, holdPatch-applies-N-times) are genuine. Not misleading, just one weak assertion + a misnamed file.

The `if (isMain) {` body (both gen-overlay-registry.mjs and gen-gallery-seed-registry.mjs) is left at column 0, not indented into the block. Harmless functionally and NOT caught by `npm run check` (which only runs biome lint --only=style/noRestrictedImports), but biome format:check / a formatter run would reflag it. Cosmetic drift.

Inconsistent gallery-seed exclusion across the three coverage gates: gen-testid-registry.mjs excludes BOTH gallery.tsx and gallery.ts, but gen-state-matrix.mjs (and gen-gallery-coverage.mjs) exclude only 'gallery.tsx'. The seed contract permits gallery.ts (hasSeed + the runtime glob both accept .ts), so a module using gallery.ts could leak into the state-matrix surface set. coverage.mjs only walks .tsx so it is moot there; state-matrix is the real gap. No gallery.ts files exist today.

walk(DIST) is invoked twice (once to build offenders, once inline in the success console.log to count assets), re-reading the whole dist tree; and the offenders filter reads every JS file fully into memory with readFileSync+includes. Negligible for a one-shot CI script, but the double-walk is avoidable by caching the file list.

Reviewed clean. File reads are existence-guarded (module.tsx/manifest/exceptions all fall back to '' when absent); JSON.parse only on the trusted committed overlay-allowlist.json; no unhandled-rejection paths in the pure functions. Only nit: walk()/enumerate use statSync without try/catch so a broken symlink in the tree would throw, which is acceptable for a build gate.

Reviewed clean. Every new gate reads its source-of-truth and allow-list from PERMANENT product-tree paths only: seed gate -> src/dev/gallery/GALLERY_SEED_MANIFEST.md + GALLERY_SEED_EXCEPTIONS.md + src/modules; overlay gate -> src/dev/gallery/overlay-allowlist.json + src/modules/*/gallery.tsx; prod-exclusion -> src-app/dist/ui build output. No gate reads from `.lifecycle/` (which is stripped at merge). Survives the strip.

Reviewed clean overall. Ran all new unit tests: 17 mjs (TEST-1..5) + 9 support .ts (TEST-6/7/8) all PASS. computeSeedDrift missing/stale, hasUserSurface route+slot+comment-strip, parseSeedExceptions reason+signoff, mergeModuleCassettes collision-throw, assertUniqueSlugs cross-class dup, and the hold.ts durability helpers are each exercised with real inputs and correct assertions (not always-true). extractWiredSurfaces union + empty-aggregator covered. Real gate runs also green: seed 39 modules/36 seeded/0 allowlisted, overlay 85 surfaces.
